title: New Hampshire Follows Bitcoin Reserve With 'Blockchain Basic Laws' Signing
summary: New Hampshire's newly-signed crypto law introduces protections for users, miners, and stakers within its boundaries.
body:
New Hampshire has taken a significant step toward embracing cryptocurrency by signing the 'Blockchain Basic Laws' into effect. This legislation provides legal protections for individuals and businesses involved in cryptocurrency activities, including mining and staking. The law aims to create a clear and supportive legal environment for blockchain technologies within the state.

The new law includes provisions that protect users from unfair practices and ensures that miners and stakers operate within a defined legal framework. It also addresses issues related to taxation and liability, providing clarity for those involved in the crypto space. Governor Chris Sununu emphasized the importance of fostering innovation while ensuring consumer protection.

For everyday people, this legislation means greater legal certainty when engaging with cryptocurrencies. It can encourage more individuals and businesses to participate in the crypto economy, knowing they have legal protections in place. The law also positions New Hampshire as a forward-thinking state in the rapidly evolving blockchain landscape.

Moving forward, other states may look to New Hampshire's example as they consider their own cryptocurrency regulations. The law's impact will be closely watched, particularly in terms of how it affects the state's economy and its attractiveness to crypto businesses. For now, residents and businesses in New Hampshire can operate with greater confidence in the legal framework surrounding blockchain activities.

The new law includes protections from unfair practices, clear taxation rules, and defined liability for miners and stakers. Governor Chris Sununu highlighted the need for innovation and consumer protection.

This legislation could encourage more people and businesses to engage with crypto in New Hampshire. Other states may follow suit. Read more → https://cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-07-14-new-hampshire-enacts-blockchain-basic-laws-to-protect-crypto-users
